title: iris tenax subsp. bracteata
intro: Iris tenax subsp. bracteata, also known as the sacred iris or bearded iris, is a subspecies of the Iris tenax plant native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Iris tenax subsp. bracteata can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential applications.
